title: Anthropic launches Claude Science, an AI workbench for lab researchers
subtitle: A coordinating agent with 60+ curated skills for genomics, proteomics, and cheminformatics — plus a $30,000 credit program for 50 research projects.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Anthropic released Claude Science, an AI workbench aimed at bench and computational scientists rather than software engineers. It ships with a coordinating agent that has access to more than 60 curated skills and connectors pre-configured for genomics, single-cell analysis, proteomics, structural biology, and cheminformatics. Users can spin up specialist sub-agents, and every run produces auditable artifacts intended to survive a peer-review workflow. The product is in beta for Claude Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise customers.

Anthropic paired the launch with a funding program: up to 50 AI-for-Science projects will receive up to $30,000 in Claude credits, with cloud partner Modal contributing up to $2,000 in compute for selected projects. Applications close July 15, awards land by July 31, and funded work runs September 1 to December 1, 2026.

The design choice underneath Claude Science is that scientists don't need a smarter model — they need a smarter workflow. Rather than debut a new bioscience-specific model, Anthropic is betting that a general model plus curated skills, connectors, and audit trails is the missing piece for laboratory adoption. It's also visibly a revenue-diversification move ahead of the IPO, staking ground in a research market where the incumbents (Isomorphic, DeepMind's AlphaFold ecosystem, Insilico) have optimized around specific molecular tasks rather than the day-to-day analysis workbench.

For learners in life sciences or research computing: the interesting job here is not writing skills, it's designing the audit artifact. A model output that a reviewer can trace back to inputs, parameters, and intermediate steps is the difference between a curiosity and a citeable result. That's a discipline the field has been asking software vendors for since ELN 1.0 — and it's now the differentiator across every scientific AI product shipping this quarter.
